why: re-open: the former gateway/ node (two fleet personas bundled) split into per-persona nodes pod/ (in-ship bridge) and operator/ (out-of-ship dispatcher). Per ADR-0022 they were always two shipped skills; this gives each its own node/.feature/design. Frozen gateway.feature retired, re-authored as pod.feature and operator.feature scoped to each persona's real behavior read faithfully from the built plugins/cyberfleet/skills/{pod,operator} SKILL.md, plus shared etiquette and each persona's half of the mode-switch. Self-asserted by:agent, directly in main at the Council's direction. Structural checks green: concept-index no drift, check-spec-structure 0/0, both persona SKILL.md pass audit validate. Non-blocking follow-up: cold ACED spec+impl judge pass over the two new nodes to re-grade @trigger balance and @rubric thresholds standalone.

no implementation changed by the split; the two persona skills (plugins/cyberfleet/skills/{pod,operator}) already built and passing audit validate, and the new nodes were authored to match them one-to-one. Root stays implemented. pod/ and operator/ remain @frozen.

note: deferred cold ACED judge pass ran over both new per-persona nodes and both passed cleanly. pod: spec-judge ALIGNED true (oracle/builder/architect all PASS, 12/12 scenarios + rubric), impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true (@trigger 8/8 = 1.0, @rubric 8.7/9 >= 7). operator: spec-judge ALIGNED true (all lenses PASS), impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true (@trigger 8/8 = 1.0, @rubric 9.0/9 >= 7). Only low/info observations, none blocking: HAL scenario collapses entry-vs-post-assert timing; untagged boolean deferral scenarios (a formation smell, check-spec-structure 0/0); Pod greeting-vs-register order slightly underspecified. No frozen scenario changed; follow-up closed.
